# Model Context Protocol (MCP)

What if you want your agent to search the web, query a local SQLite database, or interact with Slack, without writing custom tools for each?

**MCP (Model Context Protocol)** is an open standard that allows agents to discover and connect to data sources universally using an MCP Server.

## Using MCP with Agents

The OpenAI Agents SDK provides built-in utilities to connect to any MCP server and automatically convert its tools into native agent tools!

```python
from openai_agents import Agent, Runner
# Assuming the MCP client is available through standard MCP python packages
from mcp.client.stdio import StdioServerParameters, stdio_client
from mcp.client.session import ClientSession
import asyncio

async def main():
    # Define how to connect to our mock MCP server
    server_params = StdioServerParameters(
        command="python",
        args=["mock_mcp_server.py"] # We will create this in the final project
    )

    async with stdio_client(server_params) as (read, write):
        async with ClientSession(read, write) as session:
            # Initialize connection
            await session.initialize()
            
            # Fetch tools from the MCP server
            mcp_tools = await session.list_tools()
            
            # Map them to OpenAI tools
            # (Note: Specific mapping logic depends on the specific helper version, 
            # here we abstract it for the conceptual tutorial)
            agent_tools = mcp_tools # mapped tools
            
            mcp_agent = Agent(
                name="MCPAgent",
                instructions="You use the provided MCP tools to assist the user.",
                tools=agent_tools,
                model="gpt-4o"
            )

            result = await Runner.run(mcp_agent, input="Can you fetch my latest data using MCP?")
            print(result.final_output)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
```

By adding an MCP connection, your agent instantly becomes capable of interacting with standard tools across the entire AI ecosystem!
